title: dalbergia alata
intro: Dalbergia alata, also known as the golden canarywood, is a tropical tree species native to the Philippines. It is a deciduous tree that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bark and roots of the Dalbergia alata tree are rich in flavonoids, tannins, and other compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
